title: Prompt Security, Untrusted Context, Privacy, and Authorization · Failure Case: Prompt Injection
summary: Harden a tool-using prompt that processes untrusted documents and external results. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt prompt injection increment in a prompt threat model and red-team suite. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Recognize direct and indirect prompt injection and prevent authority escalation.
Separate trusted instructions from untrusted data and label authority boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
